_DRAFT_NOTES: Pre-submission draft. Pending: (1) peakVramGb measured via free -g sidecar (UMA, nvidia-smi N/A) - waiting for measurement run, (2) max-model-len 262144 stability re-verify with KV pool measurement.
notes: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-NVFP4 (RedHatAI quant, ~22 GB, sha e850c696) on DGX Spark GB10 with vLLM 0.19.2rc1.dev213+g9558f4390 + DFlash speculative decoding (drafter z-lab/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-DFlash sha 42d3b34d, num_speculative_tokens=15). Sherlock prose corpus, pp=128, tg=128, c=1, depth=0, n=30 trials (warm-pass, cold-start dropped → n=29). tokSOut = 102.05 t/s MEDIAN (mean 125.15, std 48.74). High variance is intrinsic to DFlash — drafter acceptance rate varies per token. Speedup vs NVFP4 AR baseline (42.98 t/s median, std 0.13) on identical hardware/engine = 2.37×. AR optimization investigated (FP8 KV, alt attention backends, Blackwell env-var tuning) — none beat default by >5%, MoE-A3B decode is dominated by expert weights not KV. Requires DFlash layer-tap off-by-one fix (idempotent patch in repo, applied at docker build); without it, drafter reads pre-embedding state and acceptance collapses ~80%→3%. ttftMs=153 median, contextLength=262144 (Qwen3.6 max_position_embeddings, KV pool 2.18M tokens at c=1 = 31× headroom). Reproducer (Dockerfile + scripts + raw JSONs, verified clean-from-scratch via fresh git clone + docker rebuild on a different physical Spark): https://github.com/my-other-github-account/spark-bench-reproducers/tree/main/qwen36-35b-a3b-dflash-spark — see README results table for AR baseline + 4 DFlash cells. By @banana_baeee.
